name: Auditorium Shores Off-Leash Area
description: Located along Lady Bird Lake, this popular downtown park offers open spaces with stunning skyline views and water access for dogs who love to swim. The park features well-maintained trails and plenty of room for dogs to run and socialize. It's one of Austin's most scenic dog parks with easy access to the lake.
features: Water access to Lady Bird Lake

name: Red Bud Isle
description: A scenic peninsula surrounded by Lady Bird Lake, Red Bud Isle is a paradise for water-loving dogs. This beautiful natural area features winding trails through trees and multiple lake access points. It's consistently rated as one of the best dog parks in Austin for its peaceful atmosphere and stunning natural setting.
features: Surrounded by water on three sides

name: Zilker Metropolitan Park
description: Austin's most famous park offers dedicated off-leash areas where dogs can run and play with beautiful city views. This 350-acre metropolitan park is the heart of outdoor recreation in Austin. The off-leash areas provide ample space for exercise while maintaining the park's natural beauty.

name: Walnut Creek Metropolitan Park
description: This expansive 293-acre park in North Austin features one of the largest off-leash areas in the city. With extensive hiking trails, open fields, and natural landscapes, it's perfect for dogs who love to explore. The park also includes a swimming pool and playgrounds for the whole family.

name: Norwood Estate Dog Park
description: A well-maintained neighborhood park featuring separate areas for small and large dogs, ensuring a safe and comfortable environment for all sizes. The park includes benches, waste stations, and water fountains for both dogs and their owners. It's a favorite among South Austin residents.
features: Separate areas for small and large dogs

name: Shoal Creek Greenbelt
description: This urban greenbelt offers shaded trails along Shoal Creek with a designated off-leash area between 24th and 29th streets. The tree-covered paths provide a cool respite during hot Austin summers, and the creek offers a natural water source for dogs. It's a convenient option for central Austin residents.

name: Mary Moore Searight Metropolitan Park
description: This South Austin park features a one-mile off-leash loop trail and a shallow stream where dogs can cool off. The natural setting provides a peaceful environment for both dogs and owners to enjoy nature. It's less crowded than some of the more central parks, making it ideal for dogs who prefer quieter settings.

name: Bull Creek District Park
description: Located in Northwest Austin, Bull Creek offers beautiful limestone creek beds and natural swimming holes where dogs love to play. While the park requires leashes on trails, dogs are allowed off-leash in the creek areas. The scenic greenbelt is perfect for hiking with your dog and cooling off in the water.

name: Emma Long Metropolitan Park
leashPolicy: On-Leash on trails, Off-leash in designated areas
fees: $5 per vehicle on weekends, Free on weekdays
description: Austin's largest city park at 1,147 acres, Emma Long offers extensive trails and Lake Austin access. While dogs must be leashed on trails, there are designated off-leash areas and lake access points. The park provides a more rugged, natural experience away from the city.

name: Bartholomew District Park
description: This East Austin park features a fenced off-leash area that's perfect for dogs to run and play safely. The park includes separate sections for small and large dogs, water stations, and plenty of shade. It's a community favorite with a friendly, welcoming atmosphere.
features: Separate small and large dog areas

name: West Austin Park
description: A fully-fenced 3-acre neighborhood park in the heart of West Austin featuring a dedicated off-leash area for dogs. This community favorite offers a safe environment for dogs to socialize and play. The park also includes basketball courts, tennis courts, picnic tables, and a pool for human visitors.
features: Separate areas for large and small dogs

name: Yard Bar
description: A unique combination of a bar and dog park that has become an Austin favorite. Yard Bar offers a spacious off-leash area with professional agility equipment, park attendants, and ample seating for owners. Enjoy craft cocktails and food while your dog plays in a safe, monitored environment. Perfect for socializing with both dogs and humans!

name: Onion Creek District Park
description: This expansive 517-acre park in South Austin features Dog Park Blue, an off-leash area on the northeast tip of the park. The unfenced area is close to Onion Creek, providing excellent opportunities for dogs who love to swim and cool off. The natural setting offers plenty of space for dogs to run and explore.

name: Barton Creek Greenbelt
address: 3755 S Capital of Texas Hwy, Austin, TX 78704
description: One of Austin's most beloved outdoor spaces, the Barton Creek Greenbelt spans 809 acres with nearly 8 miles of scenic trails. While dogs must remain on-leash, this greenbelt offers stunning limestone cliffs, natural swimming holes, and diverse wildlife. Perfect for hiking with your dog in a beautiful natural setting.

name: Domain Central Dog Park
description: A modern, fully-fenced dog park in the upscale Domain neighborhood of North Austin. This well-maintained park features double gates for safety, mature shade trees, and comfortable seating areas. Located in the heart of a shopping and entertainment district, it is perfect for combining a dog park visit with shopping or dining.

name: Bob Wentz Park at Windy Point
description: A stunning 211-acre park on the shores of Lake Travis in West Austin. While dogs must be on-leash, the park offers beautiful lake views, rocky shorelines, and excellent swimming spots. The park features scenic trails and is a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts and their canine companions seeking lakeside adventures.

name: Hancock Dog Park
description: A neighborhood off-leash dog park in Central Austin near the Hyde Park area. This fenced park features a mix of grassy areas and rocky terrain, providing a stimulating environment for dogs of all sizes. Shaded spots and seating areas ensure comfort for both pets and owners. Note: visitors should bring their own water as fountains may not be available.

name: Great Northern Dog Park
description: A neighborhood off-leash dog park in North Austin's Great Northern area, popular with locals who want a quieter alternative to the busier parks closer to downtown. The park features a fenced off-leash area with grassy open space, shaded seating, and waste bag stations. It tends to attract a friendly, regular crowd and has a relaxed neighborhood feel.

name: Harris Branch Dog Park
description: Located in the fast-growing Northeast Austin corridor, Harris Branch Dog Park serves the growing residential communities near Parmer Lane and Burnet Road. The park offers fenced off-leash space with open grassy areas and is a convenient option for dog owners in North Austin who want to avoid the drive to more distant parks.

name: Cedar Bark Dog Park at Elizabeth Milburn Park
address: 1901 Sun Chase Blvd, Cedar Park, TX 78613
description: One of the best dog parks in the Cedar Park area, located within Elizabeth Milburn Park just north of Austin. The park features separate small and large dog sections, both fully fenced with double-gate entry. Well-maintained grounds, shade trees, and water stations make it a comfortable all-year destination. Cedar Park residents love it for the consistent upkeep and active dog owner community.
features: Separate small and large dog areas

name: Old Settlers Dog Park
address: 3300 E Palm Valley Blvd, Round Rock, TX 78665
description: Part of the expansive Old Settlers Park in Round Rock, this off-leash dog area is one of the most popular in the northern suburbs. The park offers a large fenced space with separate sections for small and large dogs, water stations, and plenty of room to run. Round Rock's investment in park infrastructure shows here — the facilities are well-maintained and the community is active and dog-friendly.
features: Separate small and large dog sections
Part of Old Settlers Park complex

name: Steeplechase Park Dog Park
address: 1800 Gattis School Rd, Round Rock, TX 78664
description: A neighborhood off-leash dog park in Round Rock's Steeplechase community, popular with residents in the northeast Round Rock and Pflugerville border area. The park offers fenced off-leash space with grassy terrain and is a convenient local option for dog owners in this fast-growing suburban corridor. A solid neighborhood park that doesn't require a long drive.

name: Falcon Point Dog Park
description: A community dog park serving Pflugerville's Falcon Point neighborhood and surrounding areas in northeast Travis County. The park features a fenced off-leash area with open space ideal for dogs who like to run. As Pflugerville continues to grow, this park fills an important gap for dog owners in the northeast corridor who previously had to drive to Austin proper.

name: Georgetown Bark Park
description: Georgetown's dedicated off-leash dog park is a well-loved community spot in one of the fastest-growing cities near Austin. The park features separate areas for large and small dogs, both fully fenced, along with water access, shade structures, and waste bag stations. Georgetown's dog community is active and the park reflects the city's investment in quality of life amenities.
features: Separate large and small dog areas

name: Garey Park
leashPolicy: On-Leash Required on Trails; Off-Leash Area Available
description: A stunning 312-acre nature park on the San Gabriel River just west of Georgetown. Garey Park is more of a nature experience than a traditional dog park, but dogs are very welcome throughout. The park features miles of trails along the river, open meadows, and beautiful Hill Country scenery. There is a designated off-leash area, and the expansive grounds make for an exceptional outing for active dogs and their owners.

name: Buda City Park Dog Park
description: Buda's community dog park offers fenced off-leash space in this growing suburb just south of Austin on I-35. The park serves dog owners in Buda, Kyle, and the nearby Manchaca community who want a local option without driving into Austin. Well-maintained and free to use, it's a solid neighborhood resource that reflects Buda's investment in its growing community.

name: Bicentennial Park Dog Run
description: Pflugerville's Bicentennial Park includes a popular off-leash dog run area that has become a go-to for dog owners throughout the northeast suburbs. The park features fenced off-leash space alongside sports fields, playgrounds, and recreational amenities — making it a good destination for families with dogs. Pflugerville's parks system is consistently well-funded and maintained.

name: Dog House Drinkery + Dog Park
hours: Mon-Thu 3:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Fri 3:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Sat-Sun 12:00 PM - 11:00 PM
description: South Austin's beloved combination of craft beer bar and fenced off-leash dog park. Dog House Drinkery lets your dog run and socialize while you enjoy cold drinks in a relaxed, dog-forward atmosphere. The fenced dog area is supervised and well-maintained, with separate zones to keep play safe. A popular weekend hangout for the Oak Hill and Southwest Austin crowd, it perfectly captures Austin's dog-friendly culture.
features: Full bar with craft beer and cocktails
